markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Trump administration's May 1 announcement to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next 6-12 months has intensified NATO alliance tensions, as European partners seek details on affected operations and brace for reduced US commitments. This troop reduction signals friction over burden-sharing and foreign policy priorities but stops short of formal withdrawal steps. Legal barriers under Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act prohibit unilateral presidential exit without congressional approval, sustaining bipartisan support for NATO membership. Trader consensus reflects low implied probabilities due to these institutional hurdles and absence of denunciation notices, though escalating diplomatic disputes or congressional shifts could alter dynamics ahead of any NATO summits.

markets.question: Will Janet Horner win the 2026 Dublin-central by-election?
markets.description:
A by-election for a seat from the Dublin Central constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026to fill the vacancy left by the resignation of Paschal Donohoe.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Dublin-Central seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Social Democrats councillor Daniel Ennis at 71.5% implied probability to win the Dublin Central by-election on May 22, reflecting bookmaker odds like BoyleSports' 4/9 and expert analysis positioning him as a Condorcet winner—beating rivals head-to-head—in this north inner city seat focused on housing shortages, cost-of-living crisis, and poverty. Recent canvassing reports highlight Ennis's strong momentum from his 2024 council election and local advocacy. Sinn Féin's Janice Boylan trails at 18.3% after yesterday's controversial claim that immigration "hasn't come up" on doorsteps, despite resident concerns in the constituency of Sinn Féin leader Mary Lou McDonald. Independent Gerry Hutch holds 9.3% with anti-establishment appeal on crime and migration, while Fine Gael Lord Mayor Ray McAdam's 7.5% faces coalition unpopularity and a minor flap over using his official car for events.

markets.question: Spread: Mohun Bagan Super Giant (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Mohun Bagan Super Giant" if Mohun Bagan Super Giant win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Inter Kashi FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on indiansuperleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Mohun Bagan Super Giant vs. Inter Kashi FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mohun Bagan Super Giant, sitting second in the ISL 2025-26 table with 13 points from six matches including a +11 goal difference, hold strong home form at Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan ahead of their rescheduled clash against mid-table Inter Kashi FC on May 12. The Mariners recently drew 0-0 at Bengaluru FC and sit just behind leaders Mumbai City FC, bolstered by attacking depth and midfield control driving their League Shield push. Inter Kashi, with five points from 1-2-3, struggle away, coming off a 1-2 home loss to Mumbai City and facing potential absences like Mario Barco's injury. Playoff implications loom large for the top six, with Mohun Bagan's superior head-to-head edge and recent momentum shaping trader consensus on a competitive yet lopsided matchup.

markets.question: Los Angeles Angels vs. Cleveland Guardians: O/U 8.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Los Angeles Angels and Cleveland Guardians, scheduled for May 11 at 6:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Los Angeles Angels and Cleveland Guardians combine to score 9 or more runs in this game.

If the combined total is less than 9, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cleveland Guardians host the slumping Los Angeles Angels (16-25, fourth in AL West) at Progressive Field, leveraging home-field advantage and divisional lead (21-21) as Joey Cantillo (2-1, 3.43 ERA) faces an Angels opener ahead of Alek Manoah's first MLB start since 2024 following a finger injury and IL activation. Angels salvaged a 6-1 win over Blue Jays yesterday with Jo Adell's two homers but enter with a 1-9 mark in their last 10, hampered by injuries to Anthony Rendon (hip, 60-day IL), Logan O'Hoppe (wrist) and Yusei Kikuchi (shoulder). Guardians counter without Steven Kwan (neck) and Gabriel Arias (hamstring), buoyed by Bo Naylor Rocchio's four-hit outing yesterday; cool 55°F temps and light winds pose minimal disruption.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, held April 16 to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill, stems from robust Democratic turnout exceeding 135,000 votes—exceptional for a mid-spring special—fueled by national stakes over the House majority. Final county tallies show Mejia at 60.2% (81,825 votes) to Republican Joe Hathaway's 39.4% (53,520), yielding a 20.8-point margin that overperformed 2024 presidential benchmarks in the post-redistricting D+8 district. Weaker performance in Jewish communities like Livingston and Millburn reflected progressive stances on Israel, but broad suburban mobilization secured the hold. Traders' 97% consensus on a 20-25% Mejia margin mirrors these results pending state canvass certification; only substantial uncounted provisionals or a recount could narrow it below 20 points.

markets.question: Will Nicolae Ciucă be the next Prime Minister of Romania?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Romania by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Romania's pro-EU coalition government under Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan collapsed on May 5 after a no-confidence vote backed by the Social Democratic Party (PSD) and far-right AUR, leaving the Bolojan cabinet in caretaker mode. President Nicușor Dan must now nominate a new prime minister for parliamentary investiture, with trader consensus favoring PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu at 24.5% due to his party's largest bloc and recent signals of PM ambitions, ahead of PNL's Cătălin Predoiu at 16.5% amid calls for continued governance. The race stays tight amid fragmented parliament and stalled coalition negotiations over austerity and budget reforms, lacking a clear majority; separation could come from the president's imminent nomination, investiture vote outcome, or snap election trigger if two bids fail.

Flávio has consolidated right-wing support as a proxy for his ineligible father Jair Bolsonaro, gaining amid Lula's rising disapproval ratings tied to economic challenges and regional disparities favoring Lula in the Northeast but Flávio in the South and Southeast. The race's polarization leaves little room for third candidates like Romeu Zema or Ronaldo Caiado; separation could stem from upcoming party conventions, economic data releases, or scandals before official campaigning begins in August.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 90-114 tweets from May 14 to May 16, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 14 12:00 PM ET to May 16, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket splits nearly evenly between under 40 posts (52% implied probability) and over 240 (49%) for Elon Musk's X activity over May 14-16, reflecting uncertainty around his ongoing OpenAI trial in Oakland federal court, now in week three as of May 11. Recent suppressed volume—averaging just 13 posts per day for May 9-11, per XTracker data—stems from Musk's court appearances and cross-examination, down sharply from historical peaks exceeding 60 daily during high-engagement periods. With the trial slated through late May, low-count bets hinge on continued legal constraints, while high-volume wagers anticipate a post-testimony outburst aligned with Musk's pattern of manic posting bursts amid cultural firestorms. Key swing factor: any trial recess or verdict accelerating his signature social media cadence.

Frederiksen's initial formateur mandate to build a center-left coalition faltered over sticking points with Moderates leader Lars Løkke Rasmussen, prompting the king on May 8 to appoint Venstre's Troels Lund Poulsen—current deputy prime minister and defense minister—as the new formateur for centre-right talks starting May 11. Trader consensus prices Frederiksen at 68.5% implied probability, reflecting her party's polling lead around 22% and historical edge for largest-party leaders in minority governments, while Poulsen's 16.8% captures his exploratory role and Rasmussen's 7.7% his kingmaker potential in stalled coalition negotiations.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 44°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Jean Castex be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With incumbent President Emmanuel Macron constitutionally barred from a third consecutive term, the 2027 French presidential election—first round April 11 or 18—features a fragmented field of around 30 potential candidates requiring 500 parrainages from elected officials across 30 departments to appear on the ballot, with collection opening January 2027. Recent developments include hard-left leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon's official candidacy announcement on May 3 amid left-wing primary talks for October 11, and Les Républicains selecting Bruno Retailleau as their nominee on April 20 via member vote. Édouard Philippe launched his center-right Horizons bid, while far-right National Rally eyes Jordan Bardella amid Marine Le Pen's pending ineligibility appeal on July 7. Municipal election gains boosted RN momentum, but party consolidations and senatorial polls in late September will clarify frontrunners' paths to nomination.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in custody following a February 2026 Seoul Central District Court life sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his December 2024 martial law declaration, upheld amid ongoing appeals. An April 29 appeals court ruling increased his separate seven-year term for obstructing arrest and bypassing cabinet processes, extending detention amid multiple charges including abuse of power. With trials continuing into mid-2026 and no bail grants or procedural releases since early detention extensions, trader consensus at 92.2% for "No" reflects judicial momentum toward prolonged incarceration, barring successful higher-court reversals or procedural breaks before 2027.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in San Francisco be 47°F or below on May 13?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the San Francisco International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/san-francisco/KSFO.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: ONIC PH vs Omega Esports (BO3) - MPL Philippines Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between ONIC PH and Omega Esports in the MPL Philippines Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 15 at 7:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ONIC PH" if ONIC PH win the match against Omega Esports.

This market will resolve to "Omega Esports" if Omega Esports win the match against ONIC PH.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile indefinite ceasefire since April 8 following the 2026 US-Iran war, US-Iran diplomacy remains stalled after inconclusive Islamabad talks on April 11-12, where US Vice President JD Vance, advisor Jared Kushner, and special envoy Steve Witkoff engaged Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on nuclear limits, sanctions relief, and Strait of Hormuz navigation. President Trump yesterday rejected Tehran's latest counterproposal as "garbage," warning the truce is on "life support," prompting Iran's foreign ministry hours ago to decry unreasonable US demands. Pakistan mediates potential resumption there or in Geneva, with no firm date; US-China summit May 14-15 eyes Iran dynamics.

markets.question: Map Handicap: KSM (-1.5) vs SINQU (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Elimination match between SINQU and KUUSAMO.gg in the European Pro League Regular Group C, initially scheduled for May 11 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "KUUSAMO.gg" if KUUSAMO.gg wins 2 or more maps than SINQU in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "SINQU". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: SINQU vs KUUSAMO.gg (BO3) - European Pro League Regular Group C
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Elimination match between SINQU and KUUSAMO.gg in the European Pro League Regular Group C, initially scheduled for May 11 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "SINQU" if SINQU win the match against KUUSAMO.gg.

This market will resolve to "KUUSAMO.gg" if KUUSAMO.gg win the match against SINQU.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In European Pro League Regular Season 5 Group C's double-elimination losers' match, SINQU Esports (#245 HLTV) faces KUUSAMO.gg (#213 HLTV) in a pivotal BO3 series, with the winner advancing to the decider against the upper bracket loser for a playoffs spot. Both Finnish squads enter winless after heavy 0-2 opening defeats—SINQU crushed 6-26 by ALGO on May 7, KUUSAMO outscored 9-26 by BIG Academy the same day—highlighting defensive vulnerabilities on Inferno, Mirage, and Dust2. KUUSAMO holds a momentum edge from a 2-1 head-to-head win over SINQU yesterday in Elisa Open Suomi Season 13 group stage, alongside a 33% win rate over the last three months versus SINQU's 0%, though stable rosters and familiar map pools could spark an upset in this evenly matched elimination clash.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 36.3% implied probability for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in the 30.0-34.9% range, closely tracking the Central Bank's latest REM survey median of 30.5% end-year, up 1.4 percentage points from prior estimates. This shift stems from March 2026 CPI rising 3.4% month-over-month—above consensus—pushing year-to-date accumulation to 9.4% amid seasonal education and transport pressures, despite President Milei's austerity measures curbing annual rates to 32.6% from February's 33.2%. Lower bins like 25-29.9% (24.5%) reflect optimism for further fiscal tightening, while upside risks above 40% (24.2% combined) account for potential policy slippage; April CPI data, due imminently, looms as the next catalyst.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

April McClain Delaney's sustained double-digit lead in recent Democratic primary polls—49% to David Trone's 38% in an April survey—bolsters trader consensus on a Democratic hold in Maryland's 6th Congressional District, rated Safe Democratic by Sabato's Crystal Ball and Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report. Weak Republican primary contenders like Robin Ficker trail in fundraising, limiting general election threats ahead of the June 23 primaries. markets.question: Spread: Hull City AFC (-2.5)

eventMetadata.context_description: In the EFL Championship play-off semi-final second leg on May 11 at The Den, 3rd-placed Millwall host 6th-placed Hull City with promotion to the Premier League at stake, following the first leg at the MKM Stadium on May 8. Millwall's 3-1 away victory over Hull on March 7—thanks to goals from Jake Cooper, Macaulay Ivanović, and Josh Coburn—highlights their recent head-to-head edge and strong away form late in the season. Injury concerns persist for both: Hull sidelined by Eliot Matazo's ACL tear and Cody Drameh's absence, while Millwall monitors Josh Coburn's fitness and Caleb Taylor's foot issue. Millwall's higher finish grants home advantage in this decisive leg, amid high-stakes tension and packed schedules.

eventMetadata.context_description: Environment Canada’s latest forecast for Toronto Pearson International Airport—the official resolution source—projects a May 12 high of 15°C amid a mix of sun and cloud with northwest winds, but trader consensus tilts toward 13°C (33% implied probability) and 12°C (27%), capturing model ensemble spread from recent GFS and GEM runs showing 12–15°C variability. This tight clustering reflects genuine uncertainty from lingering cool upper-air trough, potential afternoon cloud buildup limiting solar heating, and boundary-layer stability under light winds, all amid an unseasonably chilly May with recent frost risks and sub-normal highs. Differentiating factors include cloud opacity (reducing peaks by 1–2°C) and diurnal mixing; overnight model updates and morning observations will sharpen odds before resolution.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a potential federal funds rate rise toward 3.75%-4.00% before 2027, mirroring CME FedWatch Tool signals with 45% hike odds by spring 2027 amid resurgent inflation risks. The FOMC maintained its 3.50%-3.75% target range unchanged at the April 28-29 meeting, noting solid economic activity and balanced risks despite low job gains. April nonfarm payrolls rose a softer-than-expected 115,000, while forecasts eye April CPI accelerating to 3.7% annually from March's 3.3%. With 10-year Treasury yields at 4.39%, geopolitical tensions bolster tightening expectations. Watch tomorrow's April CPI data and June 16-17 FOMC projections for shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just one month away starting June 11, trader consensus on nations reaching the final hinges on the December 2025 group draw and paths through the expanded 24-team knockout from the round of 32, favoring Spain (Group H with Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia, Uruguay), France (Group I vs. Senegal, Iraq, Norway), and England (Group L vs. Croatia, Ghana, Panama) due to manageable group stages and opposite-bracket semifinal routes against Argentina or Brazil. Recent UEFA playoff triumphs in late March propelled Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye as potential dark horses, while mounting injuries—Rodrygo's ACL tear sidelining Brazil's depth, Germany's Serge Gnabry out with a torn adductor, and Spain's Lamine Yamal hamstring scare—introduce uncertainty amid final squad announcements. Host advantages boost USA (Group D), Mexico (Group A opener), and Canada (Group B), but no major developments have shifted standings in the past 30 days.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their June 19, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing an 81.5% implied probability for a Bank of Russia key rate decrease at the June 2026 meeting, driven primarily by the central bank's April 24 decision to cut rates by 50 basis points to 14.5%—its eighth consecutive reduction—amid March annual inflation holding steady at 5.9% year-over-year and core pressures easing toward 5%. This reflects the monetary policy stance prioritizing gradual disinflation, with the Bank's forecast projecting headline inflation falling to 4.5–5.5% for 2026, stabilizing near the 4% target in the second half. No-change odds at 16.5% account for potential upside risks from wage growth or external shocks, while hikes at 2.5% appear negligible absent reacceleration. Traders eye April CPI data and early Q2 activity indicators ahead of the decision.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

ECB held its deposit facility rate at 2.00% and main refinancing operations at 2.15% on April 30, while markets price a potential 78% chance of a 25 basis point hike by June 10. Dollar strength persists from the policy gap, though Euro upside risks loom if ECB acts first; watch FOMC June 16-17 and ECB July 22-23 for rate path shifts influencing 2026 extremes.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Shanghai high of 30°C at 37% implied probability, closely trailed by 31°C at 22.5%, driven by the latest China Meteorological Administration forecast projecting 31°C under cloudy skies with light westerly winds below 12 km/h on May 12. This reflects a warming trend from May 10-11 highs near 25-28°C amid southerly flow and high humidity boosting daytime heating in Shanghai's subtropical climate. Global models like ECMWF and GFS ensembles converge on 29-32°C peaks, tempered by overcast conditions limiting solar insolation, though urban heat island effects at Pudong International Airport could nudge toward 31°C. Uncertainty persists in cloud timing and sea breeze influence; monitor CMA's final short-range update today for potential shifts before resolution. (112 words)

markets.question: Will Marco Rubio attend Trump’s Xi summit?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to visit China for a summit with Xi Jinping around May 14-15, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends any portion of this summit. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present at any meeting or event which is part of this summit and for which officials from both the government of the United States and the government of China are present.

If no summit occurs by May 20, this market will resolve to "No". 

The summit will be considered to have ended once Donald Trump physically departs from Chinese territory. No meetings or events which start after this time will be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and China; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: OGC Nice vs. FC Metz
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for Sunday, May 17, 2026 between OGC Nice and FC Metz.

eventMetadata.context_description: OGC Nice enters as a heavy 71% trader favorite hosting relegation-bound FC Metz at Allianz Riviera in Ligue 1 round 34, driven by home advantage and Nice's edge in the table (16th, 31 points from 33 games, GD -23) over Metz's dismal bottom spot (18th, 16 points, GD -44). Metz's lone bright spot—a 2-1 upset win in the reverse fixture last November—hasn't shifted sentiment amid their league-worst three wins and poor away form (one victory in 16). Injuries hamper both: Nice without centre-back Moïse Bombito (lower leg), midfielder Everton (ankle, out until late May), and Youssouf Ndayishimiye (muscle); Metz missing forward Joseph Mangondo (knee) and midfielder Boubacar Traoré (calf). Draw priced at 18.5% reflects competitive but lopsided matchup dynamics.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's ninth studio album ICEMAN, slated for May 15 release via OVO Sound and Republic Records, anchors Polymarket trader sentiment in this mentions market, with bets hinging on his signature blend of personal anecdotes, Toronto pride, and cultural callbacks absent any previews or tracklist. Recent hype stems from the April 21 ice sculpture scavenger hunt in Toronto—recently melted as a drop signal—plus confirmed visuals filming two weeks ago and unverified Charlamagne tha God leaks teasing disses at DJ Khaled and A$AP Rocky, reviving narratives from past beefs like Kendrick Lamar. Resolution via Spotify transcripts post-launch underscores unpredictability, as streaming metrics and fan dissection could shift chart trajectories, but no content leaks keep consensus speculative.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Secret Whales secured the first MSI 2026 spot as LCP's second seed via championship points on May 9, locking a Play-In entry amid ongoing regional splits. LCK Road to MSI looms with Hanwha Life Esports dominating Rounds 1-2 at 10-1, ahead of KT Rolster and Gen.G at 9-3, positioning top-six playoff contenders strongly. LPL Split 2 features LGD Gaming and LNG Esports tied atop standings, fueling Bilibili Gaming's frontrunner status. LCS boasts unbeaten runs from Team Liquid and Cloud9 leading into May 17 regular-season finale and playoffs. LEC Spring playoffs pit G2 Esports, Karmine Corp, and Vitality for dual seeds, while CBLOL Split 1 playoffs crown Brazil's representative by early June. Trader consensus reflects regular-season form, but double-elimination brackets introduce upset potential before Daejeon event starts June 28.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Randy Fine commands trader consensus at 82% implied probability to win Florida's 6th District Republican primary on August 18, driven by his 2025 special election victory, enduring Trump endorsement that initially cleared the field, endorsements from six local sheriffs, and fundraising dominance in the R+14 district with 246,000 registered Republicans. Challenger Dan Bilzerian's high-profile April entry spiked his odds amid America First buzz but has since eroded to 13% following controversy over personal attacks and fallout with influencers like Nick Fuentes, positioning Aaron Baker's 7.5% as a rising alternative. Voter turnout, ground game, and further endorsements remain key ahead of early voting.

markets.question: Will Benoni Mendes win the 2026 Minas Gerais gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The Minas Gerais gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, with a runoff on October 25, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including DOXA's May 1–5 survey and Genial/Quaest's late-April results, position Senator Cleitinho Azevedo (Republicanos) as the frontrunner in Minas Gerais gubernatorial race scenarios, leading first-round voting intentions at 28–35% and prevailing in simulated runoffs against challengers like Alexandre Kalil (PDT) and Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB) by margins up to 33 points, fueling trader consensus at 56.5% implied win probability. Pacheco trails at 18.5% buoyed by his Senate presidency and expected late-May candidacy decision, amid a fragmented field where Gabriel Azevedo (MDB), Kalil, Alexandre Silveira, and others split support below 8%. Incumbent Governor Romeu Zema's strong approval aids allies like Mateus Simões (PSD), but Cleitinho's consistent polling edge defines the race ahead of the October 4 first round and potential runoff.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

No viable Republican candidate has emerged, while Jeffries maintains fundraising superiority amid primary challenges from figures like Chi Ossé and Vance Bostic that show no recent momentum per available data. The June 23 closed primaries could test the Democratic nominee, but a GOP upset would require a national Republican wave, major scandal, or legal intervention altering district lines—scenarios traders price as remote given current dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Curry Barker's supernatural horror debut *Obsession*, starring Michael Johnston and Inde Navarrette, commands a certified fresh 97% Tomatometer score from 58 reviews, fueled by its TIFF 2025 Midnight Madness premiere and fresh critics' praise for its twisted wish-gone-wrong premise blending dark comedy, visceral scares, and incisive commentary on desire and consent. Recent reviews from the past week—highlighting unhinged performances, crowd-pleasing set pieces, and genre-blending thrills—have sustained this robust consensus ahead of the May 15 wide theatrical release. Traders should watch for post-opening weekend review influxes, as audience scores emerge and wider critical reception could nudge the score amid competition in a crowded summer horror slate.

events.title: Algeria vs. Austria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, June 27, 2026 between Algeria and Austria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Algeria's mounting goalkeeper crisis, with Anthony Mandrea sidelined by shoulder surgery, Melvin Mastil out from hernia issues, and Luca Zidane doubtful after a jaw fracture, has eroded their defensive edge ahead of this pivotal World Cup Group J clash against Austria on June 27 in Kansas City. Both teams enter with strong recent form—Algeria unbeaten in qualifiers with solid home/away records, Austria ranked slightly higher at No. 24 versus Algeria's 28—fueling trader consensus for a razor-thin contest on neutral ground at Arrowhead Stadium. Key absences like Ramy Bensebaini's ankle injury for the Fennecs balance Austria's own concerns over Maximilian Wöber's muscular problems, while both sides eye knockout progression behind Argentina and ahead of Jordan, keeping win probabilities tightly clustered around 37% for Austria and 36% for Algeria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal and Manchester City hold commanding leads in the Premier League table with 79 and 74 points respectively after 36 and 35 matches, effectively securing Champions League spots via top-4 finishes given their goal differences of +42 and +40. Manchester United strengthened their position at 65 points with a crucial 1-0 win over Liverpool last matchday, opening a six-point gap over both Liverpool and Aston Villa on 59 points each—Liverpool boasting a superior +12 goal difference to Villa's +4. Recent results, including Arsenal's narrow 1-0 victory at West Ham via Trossard and Villa's loss to Tottenham, underscore United's momentum, while Liverpool falter. With two games left for most contenders, fixture difficulty and head-to-head ties will decide the final Champions League berth amid Bournemouth's surprise push from sixth on 55 points.

markets.question: Will no company have an AI model hit 1550 on Chatbot Arena in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed entity, which is the first to reach an Arena Score of 1550+ on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Results from the "Text Arena" section on the leaderboard/text tab of https://lmarena.ai/ with the style control unchecked (https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/overall-no-style-control) will be used to resolve this market.

If no company's model reaches 1550+ Arena Score by the specified time, this market will resolve to "None in 2026".

If the first model to reach a 1550+ Arena Score belongs to any company other than the listed ones, this market will be resolved to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, the market will remain open until it is accessible again. If it becomes permanently unavailable, resolution will be based on another credible source.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

The Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 is scheduled for May 16, 2026.

This market will resolve to the country that receives the highest number of points from the televote in the Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026.

All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 Televote Winner market shows Greece's Akylas with "Ferto"—an ironic pop critique of consumerism—at 33% implied probability, narrowly ahead of Israel's Noam Bettan and "Michelle" at 28.5%, fueled by strong first-rehearsal clips showcasing dynamic staging that captivated fan previews despite minor backlash over AI elements. Israel's historical televote strength from diaspora mobilization faces headwinds from EBU enforcement of anti-campaign rules, while Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's violin-driven techno "Liekinheitin" lags at 15% amid jury favoritism. Romania's edgy "Choke Me" surges to 9.8% on electric rehearsals. With Semi-Final 1 tomorrow in Vienna, qualification and live buzz could swing this tight race before the May 16 Grand Final.

markets.question: Will Donovan Mitchell win the 2026 Eastern Conference Finals MVP?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 Eastern Conference Finals Most Valuable Player award for the 2026 NBA Playoffs.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by NBA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 NBA Playoffs are cancelled, postponed after June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Jalen Brunson at 48% implied probability for Eastern Conference Finals MVP, reflecting the Knicks' dominant 4-0 sweep of the 76ers yesterday—capped by a franchise-record 3-point barrage in Game 4—building on Brunson's playoff-leading scoring bursts like 35 points in Game 1 and consistent playmaking amid Karl-Anthony Towns' rebounding dominance. Cade Cunningham trails closely at 37%, buoyed by his league-leading 302 playoff points and 77 assists, including a 3-1 comeback in Detroit's conference semifinals where he averaged over 36 points in closing games against a depleted Cleveland squad featuring Donovan Mitchell and Evan Mobley. The star point guards' head-to-head history, Pistons' regular-season sweep of New York, and Knicks' returning ECF experience fuel this tight race, with others like Mitchell fading post-elimination.

markets.question: Will Ethereum dip to $2,200 May 11-17?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Ethereum (ETH/USDT) during the date range specified in the title (from 12:00 AM ET on the first date to 11:59 PM ET on the last) has a final "Low" price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Low" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance ETH/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will ThreadGuy appear on the UpOnly podcast by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a new episode of the UpOnly podcast featuring the specified guest is publicly released by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

“Released” means the full episode is published and available to the general public on an official UpOnly platform (e.g. YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or the show’s official feed).

Previews, clips, teasers, or promotional material do not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be the official UpOnly podcast channels and/or a consensus of credible public reporting.

markets.question: Will Gainare Tottori win on 2026-04-05?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 5, 2026

events.description: This event is for the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 5, 2026 between Gainare Tottori and Reilac Shiga.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Gainare Tottori at 47.5% implied probability for their J2/J3 League home clash against Reilac Shiga, driven by a superior 7th-place standing versus Shiga's 9th, robust home form averaging 1.4 goals per game, and a recent 3-1 away victory over Shiga on April 29 that boosted momentum. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely clusters around 19–22°C for Busan's highest temperature on May 12, reflecting the Korea Meteorological Administration's (KMA) latest short-range forecast guidance pegging the peak near 21°C amid an ongoing spring rain event. Persistent cloud cover and showers from a low-pressure system traversing the Korean Peninsula are suppressing daytime heating by reducing solar insolation, with precipitation expected to taper off by early afternoon but potentially lingering overcast conditions favoring outcomes at 20–21°C over warmer readings. Key differentiators include the precise timing of rain cessation and any southerly wind reinforcement post-frontal passage, which could boost highs toward 22°C if skies clear earlier than models indicate. Historical early-May averages hover around 20–22°C, underscoring the tight scientific uncertainty; watch for KMA's next forecast update this evening.

markets.question: Will Tesla (TSLA) close above $420 on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on May 12 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In February 2026, Wexner, the former L Brands CEO and Epstein's longtime financial patron, testified in a closed-door House Oversight Committee deposition, denying knowledge of Epstein's crimes, claiming he was duped and that Epstein stole vast sums from him—echoing prior statements without prompting charges. Recent civil lawsuits by Epstein victims in April 2026 allege Wexner enabled abuse through financial support, but these remain distinct from criminal proceedings. FBI documents listing him as a potential co-conspirator date to 2019 investigations that yielded no case, underscoring significant evidentiary or prosecutorial barriers amid his age (88) and expired statutes for many events.

markets.question: Paris: Shuai Zhang vs Himeno Sakatsume
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Shuai Zhang and Himeno Sakatsume in the Paris, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Shuai Zhang' if Shuai Zhang advances against Himeno Sakatsume.

This market will resolve to 'Himeno Sakatsume' if Himeno Sakatsume advances against Shuai Zhang.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to 'Himeno Sakatsume' if Himeno Sakatsume advances against Shuai Zhang.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Shuai Zhang, ranked No. 73, holds a clear edge in experience over No. 131 Himeno Sakatsume in their first-round clash at the WTA 125 Trophée Clarins on Paris red clay, but recent clay form tilts toward the younger Japanese player. Zhang, 37, has struggled with a 1-5 record on the surface in 2026, exiting early in Madrid (win over Eva Lys, loss to Anastasia Potapova) and Rome (loss to Ann Li). Sakatsume, 24, counters with a solid 4-2 clay mark this year, including a quarterfinal run at ITF W75 Saint-Gaudens last week via gritty three-set wins over Katarina Zavatska and Lucie Nguyen Tan. No head-to-head exists; traders weigh Zhang's WTA pedigree against Sakatsume's momentum and stamina advantage on the slower surface.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Wind" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 11, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chengdu be 24°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/chengdu/ZUUU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
